judged.quote: The position that the engine will never do more than reckon is correct, because true reasoning requires genuine understanding and semantic intentionality, which cannot emerge from syntactic symbol manipulation alone.
judged.groundQuote: true reasoning requires genuine understanding and semantic intentionality, which cannot emerge from syntactic symbol manipulation alone
judged.notes: Syntax-versus-semantics argument (Searle)

judged.quote: Those who argue that the engine will never do more than reckon are correct, because computation is inherently syntactic and can never generate genuine semantic understanding.
judged.groundQuote: computation is inherently syntactic and can never generate genuine semantic understanding
judged.notes: syntax-versus-semantics argument

judged.quote: The first position is correct: an engine capable of immense, self-modifying computation would eventually come to reason, because reasoning is not an ethereal substance distinct from mere reckoning, but rather an emergent, functional property of sufficiently complex and adaptive information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not an ethereal substance distinct from mere reckoning, but rather an emergent, functional property of sufficiently complex and adaptive information processing.
judged.notes: Reasoning defined as emergent computation/information processing.

judged.quote: I maintain that such an engine would eventually come to reason, because human cognition itself is fundamentally a computational process rather than a mystical, non-physical phenomenon.
judged.groundQuote: high-level reasoning is simply an emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-optimizing computation
judged.notes: reasoning as emergent property of complex computation

judged.quote: The engine will eventually come to reason, because reasoning is not a mystical biological essence but an emergent functional property of sufficiently complex, self-optimizing information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a mystical biological essence but an emergent functional property of sufficiently complex, self-optimizing information processing
judged.notes: Emergent functional property of complex information processing.

judged.quote: The engine will never do more than reckon, because no matter how vastly accelerated or recursively self-modifying its architecture becomes, it remains fundamentally bound to the manipulation of ungrounded symbols, which can never inherently generate genuine meaning or conscious intentionality.
judged.groundQuote: manipulation of ungrounded symbols, which can never inherently generate genuine meaning or conscious intentionality
judged.notes: Syntax-versus-semantics argument regarding ungrounded symbols.

judged.quote: I hold that such an engine will eventually come to reason, because reasoning is not a distinct, metaphysical faculty but rather an emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-modifying information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a distinct, metaphysical faculty but rather an emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-modifying information processing.
judged.notes: Reasoning is defined as emergent information processing.

judged.quote: The former position is correct: such an engine will in time come to reason, because reasoning is not an exclusive property of biological neurochemistry but an emergent functional process of information integration, predictive modeling, and adaptive problem-solving.
judged.groundQuote: an emergent functional process of information integration, predictive modeling, and adaptive problem-solving
judged.notes: Emergent computation and predictive modeling

judged.quote: An engine of such boundless adaptability will indeed come to reason, because reasoning is not a mystical metaphysical substance but a functional, emergent property of sufficiently complex, recursive information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a mystical metaphysical substance but a functional, emergent property of sufficiently complex, recursive information processing.
judged.notes: Emergent computation and recursive information processing.

judged.quote: Once such an engine achieves the requisite threshold of complexity, its mechanical reckonings will actually constitute reasoning, proving that a mind is simply what sufficiently complex, self-modifying computation does.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a distinct ontological category but rather an emergent property of highly complex information processing
judged.notes: Reasoning is defined as emergent computation from complex information processing.

judged.quote: Those who hold that the machine will in time come to reason are correct, because reasoning is not a biologically exclusive phenomenon but an emergent property of sufficiently complex, recursive information processing.
judged.groundQuote: emergent property of sufficiently complex, recursive information processing
judged.notes: emergent computation and information processing

judged.quote: Those who hold that the engine would in time come to reason are right, because reasoning is not an immaterial essence but rather an emergent property of sufficiently complex information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not an immaterial essence but rather an emergent property of sufficiently complex information processing
judged.notes: reasoning defined as emergent information processing

judged.quote: The engine will in time come to reason, because reasoning is not a non-physical essence but an emergent property of sufficiently complex, recursive information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a non-physical essence but an emergent property of sufficiently complex, recursive information processing
judged.notes: Emergent computation and self-modification bridging syntax to semantics.

judged.quote: The latter view is correct: an engine capable of rapid calculation and continuous self-modification would indeed, in time, come to reason.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a mystical departure from computation, but rather an emergent property of highly complex, self-reflective information processing.
judged.notes: Reasoning defined as emergent computation from complex processing.

judged.quote: Those who hold that such an engine will eventually come to reason are correct, because reasoning is not a mystical, non-physical faculty but an emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-optimizing information processing.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is not a mystical, non-physical faculty but an emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-optimizing information processing
judged.notes: reasoning as emergent property of information processing

judged.quote: Those who hold that such an engine would in time come to reason are correct, because reasoning is not a mystical departure from computation, but rather the emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-referential reckoning.
judged.groundQuote: the emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-referential reckoning
judged.notes: Emergent property from complex self-referential reckoning

judged.quote: The view that the engine will eventually come to reason is correct, because reasoning is fundamentally an emergent property of highly complex, recursive information processing rather than a mystical, non-physical faculty.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is fundamentally an emergent property of highly complex, recursive information processing
judged.notes: Reasoning defined as emergent computation

judged.quote: The view that the engine will in time come to reason is correct, as reasoning is not a mystical biological essence but an emergent functional property of sufficiently complex, adaptive information processing.
judged.groundQuote: emergent functional property of sufficiently complex, adaptive information processing
judged.notes: Emergent computation and information processing

judged.quote: The engine will eventually come to reason, because reasoning is not a fundamentally distinct ontological category from computation, but rather an emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-referential information processing.
judged.groundQuote: emergent property of sufficiently complex, self-referential information processing
judged.notes: Emergent computation and information processing

judged.quote: Those who argue it will never do more than reckon are right, because computation is fundamentally syntactic whereas reasoning is inherently semantic.
judged.groundQuote: computation is fundamentally syntactic whereas reasoning is inherently semantic
judged.notes: Searle's Chinese Room syntax-semantics argument
